questions.text: The autotrophic mode of nutrition requires:
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis (autotrophic nutrition) requires CO2, water, chlorophyll, and sunlight to produce food.

questions.text: The breakdown of pyruvate to give carbon dioxide, water and energy takes place in:
questions.explanation: Aerobic respiration, involving the breakdown of pyruvate in the presence of oxygen, occurs in the mitochondria.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct path of urine in our body?
questions.explanation: Urine is produced in kidneys, travels through ureters to the bladder, and is released via the urethra.

questions.text: The xylem in plants are responsible for:
questions.explanation: Xylem tissue is responsible for the conduction of water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant.

questions.text: Which enzyme is present in saliva that breaks down starch?
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase (also known as ptyalin) begins the digestion of carbohydrates by breaking down starch into sugar.
